mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking months are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that outweigh corporate profit resilience, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 when UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal income and inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that boost nominal corporate revenues faster than discount rate effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of co-occurring months, or the interaction term marginal predictive contribution is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery regimes, a sustained monthly increase in the industrial production index signals capacity utilization rebound and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in recovery periods with Rank IC below 0.04, or returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of months following industrial production increases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions catch up to macroeconomic output data.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over 5-year rolling windows, or negative returns dominate in greater than 50 percent of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 950 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to anticipated earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of months where HOUST drops below 950, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, further increases in the federal funds rate are already priced in, and the market interprets rate hikes as confirmation of economic resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes in tightening cycles yield negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below baseline with Rank IC below 0.02 during tightening periods.
